title: Remote Config
objectives: Feature-flag and A/B test with Remote Config
Fetch/activate wisely to avoid stale UI

sections.markdown: Firebase Remote Config is a managed feature-flag + configuration service. You set defaults in the app, override values in the console (with conditions targeting audiences), and the client fetches + activates the latest values.

rows: Force disable a broken feature instantly

sections.markdown: Remote Config's superpower: **release code once, roll it out gradually**. Ship the feature dark, enable for 1% → 10% → 100% via console. Combined with Crashlytics/Analytics you can auto-roll-back on regression.
Fetching on every screen — that flakes and burns quota. Fetch once on launch (with a fresh throttle), activate, and let the UI observe the active values.
Always set defaults in the client bundle so the first launch never shows a broken UI. Use conditions (country, app version, user property) — never target by raw user ID.

flashcards.back: Server-controlled key-value config with targeting conditions, delivered to clients.
So first-run users see a good UI before any fetch completes.
Start at 1%, monitor Crashlytics/Analytics, ramp up; keep a kill switch.

quiz.q: Remote Config is best used for…
quiz.explain: It's a managed config + flag system.

quiz.q: Best time to fetch on mobile?
quiz.explain: Fetch sparingly; use active values thereafter.

options: First launch may show a broken UI
quiz.explain: Defaults are the safety net before a successful fetch.

challenges.text: Gate a UI change behind a boolean flag with a default.
Roll out a new home layout to 10% of users; measure with Analytics.
Add a kill switch tied to Crashlytics alerts (via a rule).
Run an A/B test with Analytics conversions and choose the winner.

interviewQuestions: How do you gradually roll out a feature?
How do you run A/B tests?
